narrative:
Brazil's Supreme Court has convicted two politician brothers of orchestrating the 2018 murder of Rio de Janeiro city councilor Marielle Franco, concluding a nearly eight-year investigation into one of Brazil's most high-profile political assassinations.

## Key Convictions

João Francisco Inácio Brazão (known as "Chiquinho"), a former congressman, and his brother Domingos Inácio Brazão, a former adviser to Rio de Janeiro's court of auditors, were each sentenced to 76 years and three months in prison [1][2][4]. The Supreme Court justices unanimously concluded that the brothers masterminded the drive-by shooting that killed Franco, 38, and her driver Anderson Gomes, 39, in March 2018 [1][2][4].

The brothers were also convicted of the attempted murder of Fernanda Chaves, Franco's press officer, who survived the attack [4]. Additionally, Rivaldo Barbosa, who headed the police homicide division during the initial investigation, was convicted of obstruction of justice and corruption for receiving bribes from the Brazão brothers, though the court found insufficient evidence to prove his direct participation in the murders [1][4].

## Motive and Background

Franco, a gay Black woman and rising political star in Brazil's Socialism and Liberty Party, was targeted because she "became a highly significant obstacle to the economic and political interests of those who ordered the crime," according to Justice Alexandre de Moraes [1]. She had actively opposed housing developments in poor neighborhoods that would financially benefit organized crime groups, particularly militia organizations [1][2][4].

Justice de Moraes emphasized the brothers' deep criminal connections, stating they "didn't just have contact with the militia. They were the militia" [1][2]. The case exposed extensive links between politicians and organized crime in Rio de Janeiro [2][4].

## Previous Convictions

Two former police officers who carried out the actual shooting were convicted in 2024. Ronnie Lessa, who confessed to firing the shots, was sentenced to 78 years and nine months in prison, while Élcio de Queiroz, the driver, received 59 years and eight months [1][2][4]. The Brazão brothers were arrested in 2024 after Lessa named them as the masterminds as part of a plea deal with prosecutors [2].

## Family Response

Anielle Franco, Marielle's sister and Brazil's current minister of racial equality, described the convictions as marking "a new historic chapter in confronting political violence based on gender and race" [2][4]. She stated: "It was eight years of struggle to find out who ordered Marielle's killing and why. It was eight years fighting for full justice" [1][2][4].

## Broader Implications

The case highlighted the deep entrenchment of criminal networks within Rio's political system. Justice Cármen Lúcia, the only woman on the Supreme Court, questioned "how many more 'Marielles will Brazil allow to be murdered?'" [2]. The investigation was marked by obstruction, including destruction of evidence and frequent changes in lead investigators [4].

citations.title: Politician brothers convicted in Brazil for ordering murder of prominent councillor
citations.quote: Brothers Domingos Inácio Brazão and João Francisco Inácio Brazão were sentenced to more than 76 years each for masterminding the drive-by shooting that killed Franco alongside her driver Anderson Gomes in 2018.

citations.title: Brazil's Supreme Court Convicts Four Men in Murder of Marielle Franco
citations.quote: Two politicians and two former police officers were found guilty in the assassination of a rival, Marielle Franco, a Rio de Janeiro city councilwoman who fought corruption and violence.

citations.title: Brazilian politician brothers convicted of ordering murder of Rio city councillor
citations.quote: João Francisco Inácio Brazão, the former congressman known as Chiquinho, and the former adviser to Rio's court of auditors Domingos Inácio Brazão were sentenced to 76 years and three months in prison for the murders of Franco, 38, and her driver, Anderson Gomes, 39.

citations.title: Brazilian politicians accused of ordering murder of Rio councillor go on trial
citations.quote: The prosecution said their motive had been Franco's opposition to plans that would have 'legalised' land illegally seized by militias that control large parts of the city.

keyDevelopments: Supreme Court unanimously convicts Brazão brothers of orchestrating Franco's murder
Brothers sentenced to 76 years and 3 months each for murders of Franco and driver Anderson Gomes
Former police chief Rivaldo Barbosa convicted of obstruction and corruption but not murder
Case exposes deep ties between Rio politicians and organized crime militias
Convictions come nearly 8 years after the March 2018 assassination
Previous convictions in 2024 of two former police officers who carried out the shooting
